description: VERIFIED LIVE: LinkedIn feed via content-script DOM extraction. LinkedIn's API is partner-gated and it renders nothing logged out; its new server-driven feed hashes every CSS class, so the ONLY stable hooks are its [data-testid] markers — these tools read post text from them in your open, logged-in LinkedIn tab. Navigate to your /feed/ and let it load, then call the tool. Tested live by Claude Code: 7 feed posts' text extracted correctly. LIMITATIONS: returns post TEXT only — author name and reaction counts live in hashed-class nodes outside the stable text box and are not reliably reachable; selectors are brittle to LinkedIn's frequent feed redesigns. Requires being logged into LinkedIn.

tools.name: linkedin_feed_posts
tools.description: Text of the posts in the current LinkedIn FEED (navigate the tab to /feed/ and let it load; scroll to load more). Returns each rendered post's body text. Click 'see more' is not needed — the expandable text box holds the full text. VERIFIED (7 posts). Note: text only — no author/reactions (hashed-class nodes, not reachable).
